metadata.subject: English Language Arts
metadata.course_name: EFP Literary Studies and New Media 11
metadata.description: EFP Literary Studies and New Media 11 combines in-depth analysis of First Peoples literature with exploration of how Indigenous creators adapt and transform literary narratives into digital media formats. Students study Indigenous authors and literary traditions while examining how contemporary Indigenous artists, filmmakers, and digital creators translate literary themes into visual, audio, and interactive media. This integrated course develops critical literacy skills and creative production capabilities, enabling students to understand both traditional and contemporary Indigenous cultural expressions and to create multimedia work that honors literary traditions.
metadata.general_description: A combined exploration of Indigenous literature and digital media creation, examining how First Peoples narratives are expressed across traditional and contemporary formats.

big_ideas.statement: First Peoples literature and digital media are interconnected forms of storytelling that express Indigenous worldviews, preserve cultural knowledge, and assert Indigenous presence in contemporary culture.
big_ideas.elaboration: Indigenous literary traditions and digital creations serve similar cultural functions—maintaining knowledge, building community, resisting colonialism, and celebrating Indigenous identity. Digital formats offer new possibilities for reaching audiences while multimedia adaptations of literature honor original works while making them accessible in forms that resonate with contemporary audiences.

big_ideas.statement: Literary analysis skills and media literacy enable students to deeply understand Indigenous narratives across different formats and to recognize how adaptations shift meaning and reach.
big_ideas.elaboration: Close reading of Indigenous literature alongside critical viewing of films, videos, and digital projects develops sophisticated analytical abilities. Students learn how narrative elements translate across media, how different formats emphasize different aspects of stories, and how choices in adaptation reflect artistic vision and audience considerations.

big_ideas.statement: Creating multimedia projects based on Indigenous literature allows students to engage creatively with First Peoples narratives while developing technical and artistic skills.
big_ideas.elaboration: Adapting literature into digital formats requires deep understanding of source material while making creative choices about visual representation, pacing, sound, and audience engagement. Students become producers of Indigenous cultural content, learning to honor literary traditions while innovating within contemporary media forms.

big_ideas.statement: Indigenous media creators employ distinctive approaches that blend traditional narrative forms with contemporary technology, creating hybrid cultural expressions that serve community purposes.
big_ideas.elaboration: Indigenous artists working across literary and digital forms develop unique aesthetic and ethical approaches that prioritize cultural integrity, community benefit, and decolonial resistance. Understanding these distinctive approaches deepens appreciation for Indigenous creativity and provides models for students' own work.

categories.name: Comprehend and Connect
categories.raw: Students analyze First Peoples literature and corresponding digital media, understanding how narratives function across different formats and the connections between literary and digital cultural expression.

competencies.raw: Conduct sophisticated literary analysis of Indigenous texts, examining narrative techniques, themes, and cultural significance
i_can_statements: I can analyze narrative structure, characterization, and literary devices in Indigenous literature with depth and nuance
I can explain how Indigenous authors express worldviews and cultural values through literary choices
I can trace themes of resilience, identity, land connection, and social justice across multiple Indigenous literary works
I can discuss intertextuality and connections between different Indigenous literary works and traditions

competencies.raw: Analyze digital media adaptations and Indigenous-created digital content, understanding how narratives are transformed across formats
i_can_statements: I can identify how films, videos, or digital projects adapt or reinterpret literary narratives
I can explain what is gained and lost when stories move from literary to digital formats
I can analyze how Indigenous digital creators make choices about visual representation, pacing, and audience engagement
I can discuss the impact of different media formats on storytelling and audience experience

competencies.raw: Make connections between Indigenous literature, digital media, and broader issues of representation, cultural preservation, and decolonization
i_can_statements: I can analyze how Indigenous creators across formats address themes of cultural preservation and sovereignty
I can discuss how literature and media work together to counter stereotypes and assert Indigenous perspectives
I can explain how Indigenous storytelling in multiple formats contributes to community wellbeing and cultural continuity
I can reflect on how experiencing Indigenous narratives across formats deepens understanding and appreciation

categories.name: Create and Communicate
categories.raw: Students create multimedia projects and literary analyses, developing both critical interpretation and creative production skills.

competencies.raw: Produce written literary analyses that demonstrate sophisticated understanding of Indigenous literature and its cultural contexts
i_can_statements: I can write research essays that explore Indigenous authors, literary traditions, and thematic concerns in depth
I can develop arguments about literary meaning supported by textual evidence and cultural knowledge
I can articulate connections between literature and other forms of Indigenous cultural expression
I can communicate literary insights with awareness of audience and academic conventions

competencies.raw: Create digital media projects that adapt, respond to, or are inspired by Indigenous literature
i_can_statements: I can develop digital projects (video, multimedia, interactive media) that engage with Indigenous literary narratives
I can make intentional creative choices in adaptation that honor source material while innovating
I can incorporate Indigenous storytelling principles and visual aesthetics in digital creations
I can articulate how my digital projects demonstrate understanding of both literature and media forms

competencies.raw: Present and discuss Indigenous literature and media critically, articulating informed perspectives and engaging with multiple interpretations
i_can_statements: I can lead discussions about Indigenous literature and media, synthesizing analysis and inviting diverse perspectives
I can present research on Indigenous authors and their works across formats with cultural awareness
I can create presentations that examine literature and its digital adaptations or related media
I can engage respectfully with different interpretations while articulating my own critical perspective

areas.name: Indigenous Literary Traditions and Contemporary Literature
areas.raw: Study of First Peoples literary history, oral narrative traditions, and contemporary Indigenous authors across multiple genres and communities
topics: Oral narrative traditions and their influence on written Indigenous literature
Indigenous novelists and their major works and themes
Indigenous poets and distinctive poetic voices
Short stories and creative writing by First Peoples authors
Memoir, autobiography, and life writing in Indigenous literature
Regional and nation-specific literary traditions and voices

areas.name: Literary Adaptation and Digital Storytelling
areas.raw: Analysis of how Indigenous literary narratives are adapted into films, videos, podcasts, and interactive media; study of adaptation as creative and interpretive practice
topics: Indigenous literary adaptations into film and television
Video essays and documentary approaches to Indigenous literature
Graphic novels and illustrated adaptations of Indigenous stories
Podcasts and audio adaptations of Indigenous narratives
Interactive and immersive digital retellings of Indigenous stories
Comparing literary texts with media adaptations and discussing narrative shifts

areas.name: Indigenous Digital Storytelling and Cultural Expression
areas.raw: Exploration of how Indigenous creators use digital media to tell stories, preserve culture, and express identity; examination of distinctive Indigenous approaches to media creation
topics: Indigenous-produced films, documentaries, and video art
Indigenous social media storytelling and digital activism
Indigenous digital artists and multidisciplinary creators
Cultural protocols and ethical considerations in Indigenous media production
Indigenous creative communities and collaborative digital projects
Contemporary Indigenous youth culture and digital expression

areas.name: Representation, Resistance, and Indigenous Cultural Sovereignty
areas.raw: Critical examination of how Indigenous peoples are represented in mainstream media; analysis of how Indigenous literature and media challenge stereotypes and assert sovereignty
topics: Stereotypes and misrepresentation of Indigenous peoples in mainstream media
How Indigenous literature and media counter dominant narratives
Decolonial approaches in Indigenous storytelling
Language, identity, and cultural continuity in Indigenous narratives
Indigenous media as resistance and assertion of rights
Building Indigenous media infrastructure and distribution networks
